subject: LU-17999 lnet: prevent race in access to peer rtrcredits count

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne on RHEL 9.3/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-86vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/c48a1670-fdb1-4785-8dd7-47a5bc53311a ran 5 tests.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

I think it is possible that credit counts are positive when lnet_parse_forward_locked() checks them, but become non-positive while lnet_post_routed_recv_locked() spins in spin_lock(&lpni->lpni_lock). This can happen with MR, i.e. multiple NIs per peer: lpni_rtrcredits access is protected only with lpni_lock (rbp_credits is protected with cpt lock which should be ok).

The purpose of lnet_parse_forward_locked() checking the credit counts seems to be to trigger “eager_recv” LND handler if available. The message is then marked as “ready to be delayed” regardless if LND handler got executed or not. Only GNILND appears to have this handler. (This part is confusing to me, because I don’t understand why the same message is being put on the delay queue if GNILND is able to process it “eagerly”. Maybe this is another bug?).

If the previous two statements are correct, then perhaps lnet_post_routed_recv_locked() should be allowed to trigger “eager_recv” of the message on negative credit count?
Uploaded patch set 2: Patch Set 1 was rebased.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I think it is possible that credit counts are positive when lnet_parse_forward_locked() checks them, but become non-positive while lnet_post_routed_recv_locked() spins in spin_lock(&lpni->lpni_lock). This can happen with MR, i.e. multiple NIs per peer: lpni_rtrcredits access is protected only with lpni_lock (rbp_credits is protected with cpt lock which should be ok).

I don't understand how multi-rail is a factor here. The same cpt lock protects both lpni_rtrcredits and rbp_credits in the lnet_parse_forward_locked()->lnet_post_routed_recv() code path. I think the bug is on credit return path because the correct cpt lock may not be held.

Eager receive gives an LND the opportunity to release resources related to this message since it is going to be delayed. It does not transfer the entire payload. I agree the code could be re-worked to move the lnet_ni_eager_recv() call to lnet_post_routed_recv(), but would need to be careful that the call occurs before message is placed on either credit list, and need to ensure that it is only called once. However, this doesn't solve the problem that wrong cpt lock is held on credit return path.

Patch Set 2:

> I don't understand how multi-rail is a factor here. The same cpt
 > lock protects both lpni_rtrcredits and rbp_credits in the
 > lnet_parse_forward_locked()->lnet_post_routed_recv() code path. I
 > think the bug is on credit return path because the correct cpt lock
 > may not be held.

My understanding is that the same lpni can be accessed under different cpt locks as a result of handling messages from the same peer NID received on different local NIs. This can cause lpni_rtrcredits to change when current code doesn't expect.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > I don't understand how multi-rail is a factor here. The same cpt
>  > lock protects both lpni_rtrcredits and rbp_credits in the
>  > lnet_parse_forward_locked()->lnet_post_routed_recv() code path. I
>  > think the bug is on credit return path because the correct cpt lock
>  > may not be held.
> 
> My understanding is that the same lpni can be accessed under different cpt locks as a result of handling messages from the same peer NID received on different local NIs. This can cause lpni_rtrcredits to change when current code doesn't expect.

Ah, you are talking about if the local NIs have been assigned an explicit CPT list? Yes, that looks like another bug. This would impact rbp_credits manipulation as well.

Patch Set 2:

> This would impact rbp_credits manipulation as well.

I was mistaken about this. The router buffers are per-cpt so there is no issue there.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > This would impact rbp_credits manipulation as well.
> 
> I was mistaken about this. The router buffers are per-cpt so there is no issue there.

And because these buffers are per-cpt I think I was wrong in my theory about incorrect CPT on credit return path.

All messages on a given rbp_msgs list will have the same msg_rx_cpt, and this is held on decommit path.

So did the system that hit ASSERT assign explicit CPTs to local NIs? Otherwise I'm not sure how assert is hit.

Patch Set 2:

> So did the system that hit ASSERT assign explicit CPTs to local
 > NIs? Otherwise I'm not sure how assert is hit.

Yes, that's how they had it set: 
options lnet networks="o2ib0(ibs10)[0],o2ib1(ibs10)[0],o2ib2(ibs11)[1],o2ib3(ibs3)[0],o2ib3(ibs6)[0],o2ib3(ibs4)[1],o2ib3(ibs7)[1]" forwarding=enabled

I'm going to go ahead with the plan of eliminating the window for peer rtrcredit count to change between checks on "the parse_forward" path and update this patch accordingly

parents.subject: LU-15899 mdt: mdt_hsm_release default lov buffer

commit.subject: LU-17999 lnet: prevent race in access to peer rtrcredits count
commit.message:
LU-17999 lnet: prevent race in access to peer rtrcredits count

Refactor lnet_parse_forward_locked and lnet_post_routed_recv_locked
to have the code which checks and acts on peer rtrcredits in a single
spot, in order to avoid the race when the count is decremented
(by another thread) after being checked initially for the purpose of
"eager receiving" the message, which might cause an assert on
msg_rx_ready_delay to get triggered.

This race is possible if messages from the same peer NID are being
processed on different local NIs mapped to different CPTs.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: Serguei Smirnov <ssmirnov@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Ibe938882a69d860554cd9c875403bfb0399df8ec
